{
  "name": "square-free morphisms and entropy compression",
  "seminal_paper": {
    "arxiv_id": null,
    "doi": null,
    "citation": "A. Thue, \"Über unendliche Zeichenreihen\", Norske Videnskabers Selskabs Skrifter I. Mat.-Naturv. Klasse 7 (1906), 1-22.",
    "year": 1906
  },
  "home_domain": "combinatorics on words",
  "what_it_does": "Square-free morphisms give explicit infinite words avoiding adjacent repeated blocks, typically by proving that a substitution preserves square-freeness. Entropy compression gives probabilistic-existence and algorithmic constructions for avoiding forbidden word patterns under local dependency constraints.",
  "input_requirements": [
    "a word encoding in which the target configurations are forbidden adjacent squares",
    "freedom to construct or resample symbols of the word",
    "a finite alphabet or controlled finite dependency structure",
    "local forbidden-pattern constraints amenable to morphic preservation or entropy-compression bounds"
  ],
  "output_signature": "an infinite word avoiding the specified repeated-block patterns, or a constructive existence theorem for such a word",
  "input_match": [
    {
      "requirement": "a word encoding in which the target configurations are forbidden adjacent squares",
      "match": "YES",
      "evidence": "Prior slot A2: with g_k = n_{k+1}-n_k, an erdos_938 triple is exactly an adjacent repeated letter g_k g_k."
    },
    {
      "requirement": "freedom to construct or resample symbols of the word",
      "match": "NO",
      "evidence": "Prior slot A2 obstruction: the powerful-number gap word is fixed and arithmetically generated."
    },
    {
      "requirement": "a finite alphabet or controlled finite dependency structure",
      "match": "NO",
      "evidence": "Prior slot A2 obstruction: the gap word has an infinite alphabet and no supplied finite dependency model."
    },
    {
      "requirement": "local forbidden-pattern constraints amenable to morphic preservation or entropy-compression bounds",
      "match": "PARTIAL",
      "evidence": "Prior slot A2 gives a local repeated-letter obstruction, but no morphism, resampling process, or dependency bound is available."
    }
  ],
  "known_obstructions": [
    "The word is fixed rather than constructed.",
    "The alphabet of gap sizes is unbounded.",
    "Morphic square-free methods preserve avoidance under substitutions, but no natural substitution structure is supplied by powerful numbers.",
    "Entropy compression requires random choices or resampling dependencies, which are absent from the deterministic gap sequence."
  ],
  "fit_score": 0.375,
  "source_analog_id": "A2"
}
